events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection determination directly to `/app/answer.json`. The key code issue is the NEC bend limit between pull points for EMT, which makes this a fail at rough-complete.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 360,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.96,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"EMT run has more than 360 degrees of bends between pull points\",
    \"Conductor pulling path exceeds NEC bend limit and may damage conductors or prevent compliant pulling\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Add an accessible pull point or junction box to break the run\",
    \"Rework conduit bends so no segment between pull points exceeds 360 degrees\",
    \"Verify conductor fill and pull path after rework before cover or closeout\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed EMT installation exceeds the maximum total bend allowance between pull points. NEC requirements for EMT limit the sum of bends between pull points to not more than 360 degrees, so the rough-complete installation is non-compliant and must be corrected before inspection acceptance.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC Article 358.26 - Bends: Number in One Run\",
    \"NEC Chapter 9 - Conductor fill requirements\"
  ]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"
→ /bin/sh: 31: jq: not found
